results.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Obstacle Avoidance in Dense Urban Canyons
Multi-Sensor Fusion for Reliable GPS-Denied Navigation in City Environments
A Survey of Sense-and-Avoid Systems for Autonomous Drone Operations

pages.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Obstacle Avoidance in Dense Urban Canyons
pages.content: Authors: Jane Doe, John Smith. Abstract: We present a deep reinforcement learning approach for navigating small unmanned aerial vehicles through cluttered urban canyons. Our method uses a lightweight convolutional neural network to process depth images from an onboard stereo camera and outputs velocity commands in real time. We train the policy in a high-fidelity simulation environment featuring realistic building geometries and wind disturbances, then transfer it to a custom quadrotor platform. In extensive real-world tests across downtown Chicago and Manhattan, the drone successfully avoided static obstacles (buildings, streetlights) and dynamic obstacles (pedestrians, other drones) in 95% of trials, significantly outperforming classical potential-field methods. We also introduce a novel curiosity-driven exploration bonus that speeds convergence in sparse-reward settings. The paper provides ablation studies on network depth, sensor noise, and latency, showing robustness to 200ms control delays. This work advances the state of the art in fully autonomous urban drone delivery and inspection missions. Full paper includes detailed methodology, network architectures, and video demonstrations of flight tests.

pages.title: Multi-Sensor Fusion for Reliable GPS-Denied Navigation in City Environments
pages.content: Authors: Alice Chen, Bob Lee. Abstract: Global positioning system outages are common in urban environments, where tall buildings block satellite signals and create multipath interference. This paper proposes a robust multi-sensor fusion framework that integrates visual-inertial odometry, lidar-based localization, and ultra-wideband ranging to achieve drift-free navigation without GPS. We design a factor-graph optimization backend that can dynamically re-weight sensors based on environmental confidence metrics (e.g., corner feature richness, lidar point cloud density). Extensive experiments in the UrbanLoco dataset and new custom data collected in San Francisco’s financial district demonstrate that our system maintains sub-meter positioning accuracy over 2 km trajectories, even in tunnels and parking garages. We also release our sensor fusion software as open-source for the community. Key contributions include a novel online covariance estimation technique for UWB anchors and a failure detection algorithm that gracefully handles sensor degradation. The paper covers system calibration, real-time performance benchmarks on an NVIDIA Jetson, and comparisons against state-of-the-art SLAM systems.

pages.title: A Survey of Sense-and-Avoid Systems for Autonomous Drone Operations
pages.content: Authors: Maria Garcia, Alan Turing. Abstract: This survey reviews over 80 papers on sense-and-avoid technologies for autonomous drones, covering computer vision, radar, acoustic, and cooperative surveillance approaches. We categorize existing work by sensor modality, detection range, computational cost, and regulatory compliance. Special emphasis is given to urban scenarios, where the density of obstacles and presence of non-cooperative aircraft pose unique challenges. We identify key gaps: limited perception at night, high false positive rates on small objects, and the lack of standardized benchmarks that mix static and dynamic threats. The paper concludes with a roadmap for future research, advocating for hybrid systems that fuse multiple sensors and leverage edge AI accelerators. An appendix provides a comprehensive table comparing datasets such as UAVDT, VisDrone, and our newly introduced CityScape-Air set, which includes annotated flights over 12 km of Manhattan rooftops.
